subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Eta Carinae: A Ticking Time Bomb
description: A short lesson to help you apply Eta Carinae: A Ticking Time Bomb.

hook.text: Eta Carinae is 5 million times brighter than the Sun and dangerously unstable. It could explode at any time.

buildup.text: In 1843, it erupted so violently it became the second-brightest star in the sky—without actually exploding.

discovery.text: The eruption ejected 10 solar masses of material, forming the Homunculus Nebula—two glowing lobes around the star.

twist.text: Eta Carinae is actually two stars orbiting each other. Their winds collide at millions of degrees.

climax.text: When it finally goes supernova, it may produce a gamma-ray burst—one of the most energetic events possible.

punchline.text: Already erupted once. The real explosion hasn't happened yet.

quiz.question: What happened during Eta Carinae's Great Eruption in 1843?
options: It ejected massive amounts of material without actually exploding as a supernova
It collapsed into a black hole that is still growing
It split into two separate stars
